subject: Mathematics
overallDescription: 6 units extracted from the curriculum source using staged structural extraction.
coreObjectives: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.
Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.

topics: Introduction to rational numbers
Representation of rational numbers on the number line
Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
Decimal representation of rational numbers
Introduction to irrational numbers
Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3
The square root spiral

competency_groups.title: Understanding of Numbers and Real Number Properties
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Prove the irrationality of √2 and √3.

competency_groups.title: Computational Thinking in Number Systems
competency_groups.description: Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.
competencies: Generate decimal expansions systematically using step-by-step logical procedures.

competency_groups.title: Algebraic Modelling and Problem Solving
competency_groups.description: Models real-life situations in the form of equations to solve them.
competencies: Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.
Define a polynomial and identify its degree, terms, and coefficients.
Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.
Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.

competency_groups.title: Linear Equations Modelling and Graphing
competency_groups.description: Models and solves contextualised problems using equations.
competencies: Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a linear equation in two variables.

competency_groups.title: Linear Equations in Two Variables: Solving and Modelling
competency_groups.description: Solve contextualised problems using pairs of linear equations.
competencies: Graph a pair of linear equations.
Solve a pair of linear equations graphically, by substitution, or elimination.
Determine the nature of solutions (consistency/inconsistency).
Model and solve contextualised problems using pairs of linear equations.

competency_groups.title: Sequences and Progressions Modelling
competency_groups.description: Models patterns using sequences and progressions.
competencies: Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.
Identify patterns in sequences and predict terms.
Determine recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.
Obtain terms given recursive or explicit rules.

competency_groups.title: Arithmetic and Geometric Progressions Analysis
competency_groups.description: Analyse attributes of APs and GPs.
competencies: Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP) and determine the nth term.
Visualise an AP graphically.
Identify Geometric Progressions (GP) and determine the nth term.
Visualise a GP graphically.

competency_groups.title: Advanced Sequence Applications
competency_groups.description: Apply sequences to fractals and puzzles.
competencies: Analyse attributes of fractals using GP.
Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle.

competency_groups.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities
competency_groups.description: Use computational thinking to visualise algebraic identities.
competencies: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.

competency_groups.title: Factorisation and Simplification Strategies
competency_groups.description: Use computational thinking strategies for factorisation.
competencies: Determine factors of algebraic expressions using identities.
Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models (with/without tiles).
Find simplified versions of rational expressions.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry and Spatial Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Specify locations and describe spatial relationships using coordinates.
competencies: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.
Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.
Determine whether three points lie in a straight line.
Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment.

competency_groups.title: Geometric Verification via Coordinates
competency_groups.description: Apply computational thinking to verify geometric properties.
competencies: Check whether a triangle is right-angled using coordinates.

competency_groups.title: Euclid's Axioms and Constructions
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements using axioms and postulates.
competencies: Describe the history of geometry (India, Egypt, Greece).
Understand definitions, axioms, and postulates.
State Euclid's 5 postulates.
Define parallelism.
Explain constructions from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Lines and Angles Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Apply computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.
competencies: Explain notions of angle and ray.
Classify angles (acute, right, obtuse, reflex).
State and apply linear pair theorem and converse.
Follow proof by contradiction in geometry.

competency_groups.title: Theorems on Intersecting and Parallel Lines
competency_groups.description: Justify geometric results through logical reasoning.
competencies: Prove vertically opposite angles are equal.
Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.
Explain transitivity of parallelism.

competency_groups.title: Triangle Properties and Rigidity
competency_groups.description: Explain triangle rigidity and congruence.
competencies: Explain that a triangle is rigid unlike a quadrilateral.
Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.

competency_groups.title: Congruence Conditions and Proofs
competency_groups.description: Use congruence axioms to prove properties.
competencies: Identify correspondence between vertices, sides, and angles of congruent triangles.
Use SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions.

competency_groups.title: Propositions and Converse Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Explain propositions and their converses.
competencies: Use counter examples to show that some converses are false.
Identify situations where SSA is a valid congruence condition.

competency_groups.title: Quadrilaterals and Parallelograms
competency_groups.description: Prove characterisations of parallelograms.
competencies: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.

competency_groups.title: Midpoint Theorem and Symmetry
competency_groups.description: Prove midpoint theorem and understand symmetry.
competencies: Prove the midpoint theorem and its converse.
Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons.

competency_groups.title: Circle Definitions and Properties
competency_groups.description: State definitions and explain circle properties.
competencies: Explain meanings of chord, diameter, radius, arc, segment, sector.
Construct circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.

competency_groups.title: Circle Theorems on Chords and Angles
competency_groups.description: Explain relationships between chords, arcs, and angles.
competencies: Explain why equal chords subtend equal angles at the centre.
Explain perpendicular bisector properties of chords.

competency_groups.title: Circle Theorems on Segments and Cyclic Points
competency_groups.description: Explain angles in segments and cyclic quadrilaterals.
competencies: Explain why angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double that at circumference.
Determine when four points are concyclic.

competency_groups.title: Area and Perimeter Concepts
competency_groups.description: Define perimeter and explain circle properties.
competencies: Define perimeter as the length around a boundary.
Explain that circumference-to-diameter ratio is constant.

competency_groups.title: Heron's Formula and Historical Context
competency_groups.description: Compute area using Heron's formula.
competencies: Explain why a median divides a triangle into two equal areas.
Use Heron's formula to compute the area of a triangle.

competency_groups.title: Brahmagupta's Formula and Generalisation
competency_groups.description: State Brahmagupta's formula.
competencies: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a shape.
Compute area using Heron's and Brahmagupta's formulas.

competency_groups.title: Sector Area and Computational Thinking in Mensuration
competency_groups.description: Compute sector areas and use computational thinking.
competencies: Explain and use the formula for area of a sector.

competency_groups.title: Surface Area and Volume Calculations
competency_groups.description: Compute surface areas and volumes of 3D shapes.
competencies: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres in real life.

competency_groups.title: 3D Shape Analysis via Computational Thinking
competency_groups.description: Systematically calculate and compare 3D shapes.
competencies: Compute surface area and volume of cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres.

competency_groups.title: Statistical Data Analysis
competency_groups.description: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data.
competencies: Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.

competency_groups.title: Graphical Representation of Data
competency_groups.description: Create appropriate graphical representations.
competencies: Read and interpret stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs.

competency_groups.title: Probability Concepts and Scale
competency_groups.description: Understand randomness and probability scale.
competencies: Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.

competency_groups.title: Empirical and Theoretical Probability
competency_groups.description: Estimate empirical probability.
competencies: Define theoretical probability of an event.

competency_groups.title: Probability Modelling with Diagrams
competency_groups.description: Compute probability using tree diagrams.
competencies: Apply computational thinking strategies (pattern recognition, simulation) to model random experiments.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks
competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units with emphasis on computational thinking (CG-9), mathematical modelling (C-3.2/C-8.1), and geometric proofs (C-7.1/C-7.3).
Specific competencies mapped to chapters include: C-1.1, C-3.1, C-3.2, C-4.1, C-4.2, C-5.1, C-6.1, C-6.2.

bloom_levels.level_1: Remembering: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts.
bloom_levels.level_2: Understanding: Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.

bloom_levels.level_3: Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

bloom_levels.level_4: Analysing: Examine and break information into parts.
bloom_levels.level_5: Evaluating: Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information or quality of work.
bloom_levels.level_6: Creating: Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern.

internal_assessment.description: Continuous assessment of work samples and projects.

practical_assessment.description: Activities to be done from prescribed books (Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools).

prescribed_books: Mathematics - Textbook for class IX - NCERT Publication
Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX - CBSE Publication
Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage - NCERT Publication
Mathematics exemplar problems for class IX, NCERT publication

project_assessment.description: Integrated into Internal Assessment as a portfolio component.

project_assessment.note: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects forms an integral part of the learning process.

outcomes: Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment using coordinates.
Find the coordinates of the fourth vertex of a parallelogram given the other three.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: History of geometry
outcomes: Describe how geometry grew from the practical needs ancient civilisations.
Describe contributions of India, Egypt and Greece to the development of geometric ideas.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
outcomes: Explain the construction of a square as given in the Sulbasutras.
Justify simple constructions using the axioms.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Discovering Euclid's definitions
outcomes: Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.
Explain that there are elements of plane geometry (point, line, surface) for which we have an intuitive sense.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Axioms: Axioms of measurement and rules for geometric objects
outcomes: State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.
Define parallelism of straight lines.

learning_outcomes.topic: Rays and angles
outcomes: Explain the notion of an angle.
Explain the notion of a ray.

learning_outcomes.topic: Measures of angles
outcomes: State that a straight angle equals two right angles and measures 180° while a right angle measures 90°.
Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.

learning_outcomes.topic: Intersecting lines and angles
outcomes: Define parallelism.
State and apply the linear pair theorem and its converse.

learning_outcomes.topic: Pairs of angles
outcomes: Follow proof by contradiction in geometry.
Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems and examples on intersecting lines
outcomes: Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.
Explain transitivity of parallelism.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems and examples on parallel lines
outcomes: Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.
Explain transitivity of parallelism.

learning_outcomes.topic: Practical applications of triangles
outcomes: Explain that a triangle is rigid, unlike a quadrilateral.
Identify uses of triangle rigidity.

learning_outcomes.topic: Proofs of conditions of congruence of triangles
outcomes: Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.
Identify correspondence between the vertices, sides, and angles of two congruent triangles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems on triangles
outcomes: Use the SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS congruence conditions.
Prove the basic properties of isosceles triangles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Propositions and their converse
outcomes: Explain the notion of a proposition.
Identify the converse of a given proposition.

learning_outcomes.topic: Problems based on applications of theorems on triangles
outcomes: Explain why not all converses are true; use counter examples to show that some converses are false.
Justify the role of diagram accuracy.

learning_outcomes.topic: Properties of parallelograms
outcomes: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.

learning_outcomes.topic: Important theorems related to parallelograms and their proof
outcomes: Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.
Find the coordinates of the midpoint of a line segment given its end points.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoint theorem and its applications
outcomes: Prove the midpoint theorem.
Find the coordinates of the fourth vertex of a parallelogram given the other three.

learning_outcomes.topic: Understanding the notion of central symmetry in the context of parallelograms
outcomes: Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons.
Practice forming logical converses of statements.

learning_outcomes.topic: Practical applications and uses of circles
outcomes: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.
Identify cultural motifs involving circles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Definitions related to a circle — centre, diameter, and radius
outcomes: State the definition of a circle.
Explain the meanings of the terms 'chord', 'diameter', 'radius', 'arc', 'segment', and 'sector'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Chords and the angles they subtend
outcomes: Explain why 'equal chords subtend equal angles at the centre'.
Explain why 'chords that subtend equal angles at the centre are equal'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors of chords
outcomes: Explain why 'the line from the centre of a circle to the midpoint of a chord is perpendicular to the chord'.
Explain why 'a perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Distance of chords from the centre
outcomes: State the relationship between length of a chord and its distance from the centre of the circle.
Explain why 'equal chords are equidistant from the centre (and conversely)'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Subtended angles by an arc
outcomes: Explain what 'angle subtended by an arc at the centre' means.
Explain why 'the angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by the arc at any point on the remaining part of the circle'.
Explain why 'angles in the same segment of a circle are equal'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Cyclicity of points
outcomes: Determine when four given points are concyclic.
Explain why 'a quadrilateral with supplementary opposite angles is cyclic, and conversely'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Perimeter of shapes
outcomes: Define perimeter as the length around the boundary of any shape.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes, apply step-by-step methods.

learning_outcomes.topic: Perimeter of a circle: Introduction to Pi and its irrationality
outcomes: Explain that the circumference-to-diameter ratio is constant for all circles.
List historical approximations to π.

learning_outcomes.topic: Length of an arc
outcomes: Compute the circumference of a circle and the length of an arc.
Apply ideas of circle perimeter and arc-length to real-world contexts.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles
outcomes: Explain why a median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of equal area.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heron's formula
outcomes: Use Heron's formula to compute the area of a triangle from its sides.
Explain why Heron's formula is a 'special case' of Brahmagupta's formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
outcomes: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of a circle: derivation
outcomes: Explain how ancient civilisations approximated the area of a circle.
Compute the area of a circle using the formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of the sector of a circle
outcomes: Explain and use the formula for area of a sector of a circle.
Solve problems on areas of sectors and segments of circles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Brahmagupta's formula for area of a cyclic 4-gon
outcomes: State Brahmagupta's formula for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral in terms of its sides.
Explain why Heron's formula is a 'special case' of Brahmagupta's formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heron's formula as a special case of Brahmagupta's formula
outcomes: Explain the notion of 'special case' and 'generalisation' in mathematics.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes, apply step-by-step methods.

learning_outcomes.topic: Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres) and right circular cones
outcomes: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.
Compute the surface area and volume of these shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.
outcomes: Explain how a cube is a 'special case' of a cuboid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a cylinder.

learning_outcomes.topic: Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a cone.

learning_outcomes.topic: Recognise a pyramid, and identify its base and apex.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a pyramid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Recognise spheres in real-life situations.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a sphere.

learning_outcomes.topic: Graphical representation of data
outcomes: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question.
Read and interpret stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs.

learning_outcomes.topic: Measures of central tendency
outcomes: Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.
Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Concept of probability and randomness
outcomes: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.

learning_outcomes.topic: The probability scale
outcomes: Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.
Estimate the empirical probability of the occurrence of an event by analysing statistical data.

learning_outcomes.topic: Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments
outcomes: Estimate the empirical probability of the occurrence of an event by analysing statistical data.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theoretical probability: sample space and events
outcomes: Define theoretical probability of an event.
Apply the definition of theoretical probability to compute the probability of an event.

learning_outcomes.topic: Representing probability through tree diagrams and tables
outcomes: Compute probability of events with the help of tree diagrams and tables.
Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation.

projects.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
projects.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules and patterns in sequences.

projects.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities with Geometrical Models
projects.details: Use geometrical models to visualise algebraic identities and factorisation of quadratic expressions.

projects.title: Squaring a Rectangle using Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
projects.details: Explore the proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras for squaring a rectangle.

projects.title: Constructing Geometrical Shapes using Axioms
projects.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
projects.details: Justify simple constructions using axioms, including constructing a square as described in the Sulbasutras.

projects.title: Discovering Geometric Patterns with Paper Manipulation
projects.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

projects.title: Identifying Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
projects.details: Identify cultural motifs such as Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

projects.title: Analyzing Real-Life Data for Decision Making
projects.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question using computational thinking.

projects.title: Simulating Random Experiments for Probability Estimation
projects.details: Use pattern recognition and simulation strategies to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.

practicals.title: Constructing the Square Root Spiral
practicals.details: Construct the square root spiral to understand irrational numbers.

practicals.title: Representing Rational Numbers on Number Line
practicals.details: Represent rational numbers and explore density through visual models.

practicals.title: Constructing a Square with Given Side (Sulbasutras)
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Perform the construction of a square as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions (Bisectors, Triangles)
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construct different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons.

practicals.title: Circumcircle Construction for Triangles
practicals.details: Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.

dependent_topics: Representation of rational numbers on the number line

dependent_topics: Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers

Slope and y-intercept of a line y = ax + b

dependency_graph.topic: Explicit or general rule of a sequence

dependent_topics: Factorisation of algebraic expressions using identities

dependency_graph.topic: Linear Equations in Two Variables: Graphical representation
prerequisites: Slope and y-intercept of a line
dependent_topics: Pair of linear equations in two variables
Distance between two points in the 2-D plane

dependent_topics: Circle Theorems on Chords and Angles
